learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 1 of the Term 1-2 sequence - standalone Digital Literacy lesson, followed by an approximately 8-lesson PowerPoint unit (beginning with Mind Maps)
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson begins the PowerPoint unit, starting with Mind Maps, building towards the 'About me' presentation golden thread for the term.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: As a student, I want to know the basic digital skills I need to work well in computer science and what I can and cannot do in the CS rooms, so that I can be successful in the course
assessment_criteria: Be able to log on and log off of  a terminal in the computer room
Be able to lock and unlock their sessions
Will be able to state the code of conduct in the CS room and terminal discipline
Open OneNote from different sources
Demonstrate that they can access their notebook in OneNote
Add data to their OneNote
Show they can add images and screenshots to their OneNote
Demonstrate they can open a link in their OneNote
Show that they can save files to their OneDrive
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: We will use teacher led demonstrations and class engagement to ensure that the basic skillsets are learned. We will get the students to update a digital literacy passport (format under review - a physical booklet or a OneNote section) as a personal record of how they did things and a verification that the student can do what they are required to do.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Explain course expectations and lab conduct rules (equipment use, food/drink, safety, terminal discipline); point pupils to the posted rules sheet.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Listen to the rules briefing and ask clarifying questions; note key expectations.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Describe and demonstrate each digital literacy skill in turn on the display screen: logging in/out, locking/unlocking, opening OneNote via Teams and the web, adding text, adding images and screenshots, opening a link, and saving to OneDrive.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch the full demonstration without attempting the steps themselves yet.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Circulate while pupils replicate each demonstrated skill in turn, checking progress and prompting pupils who fall behind.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Replicate each skill on their own PC: log in/out, lock/unlock, open OneNote, add text/images/screenshots, open a link, save to OneDrive.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Launch the Blooket quiz on lab rules and conduct; review results and address common errors.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Complete the Blooket quiz individually to demonstrate understanding of lab rules and conduct expectations.

check_for_understanding.decision_rule: No individual pass threshold; if the aggregate class score on a given question falls below 60%, that section is reviewed with the whole class.

key_vocabulary.definition: A personal computer; the individual desktop or workstation pupils log into in the CS lab.
A period of logged-in activity on a PC, ended by logging out or locking the screen.
Microsoft's cloud storage service used to save and access files from any device.

version_history.summary: Synthesised from Reference/7Term1and2Objectives.md plus teacher clarifications: full teaching_episode breakdown (60 min across introduction/provide_model/continued_learning/review_of_learning, no Do Now for this lesson), duration corrected 50->60, Blooket quiz with 60% aggregate-score review rule, learning_sequence_context filled in (Digital Literacy stands alone before the ~8-lesson PowerPoint/Mind Maps unit), digital literacy passport format flagged as under review, vocabulary Terminal->PC (plus Session, OneDrive added) and resources renamed to match, dok_level and rosenshine_principles removed pending a post-segment-development reassessment pass.

dok_level.rationale: Some students may have very limited digital skills and some may have more, DOK level 2 would be the highest we would want for them.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Describe the layout of the room, describe the terminal, demonstrate log in and log out while discussing the processes that occur.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: log in and out of their terminals
